perturbation.original_text: 1.1 "Affiliate" means any person or entity that controls, is controlled by or is under common control with the specified person or entity, but only so long as such control exists. The term "control" means the possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of an entity, whether through ownership of voting securities, by contract or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 "Affiliate" means any person or entity that controls, is controlled by or is under common control with the specified person or entity, but only so long as such control exists. The term "control" means the possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of an entity, solely through ownership of voting securities.
perturbation.explanation: By modifying the definition of 'control' in the definition of 'Affiliate', we introduce an in-text contradiction. Originally, 'control' could be established through ownership of voting securities, by contract, or otherwise. The modified text limits 'control' solely to ownership of voting securities. This perturbation impacts how Affiliate is determined, potentially creating uncertainty in Sections dealing with Affiliate actions and responsibilities throughout the agreement where they refer to the original definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1 "Affiliate" means any person or entity that controls, is controlled by or is under common control with the specified person or entity, but only so long as such control exists. The term "control" means the possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of an entity, solely through ownership of voting securities.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'control', limiting it 'solely through ownership of voting securities', while the contradicted text (which is the same definition prior to the change, but exists in other locations of the document) includes other means of control ('by contract or otherwise'). This creates a direct contradiction as the definition is now inconsistent across the contract, impacting how 'Affiliate' status is determined. This qualifies as an Ambiguity-In Text Contradiction, as definitions are inconsistent.

perturbation.original_text: 1.21 "Products" means the ENERGOUS products set forth in Exhibit A, as such Exhibit may be amended from time to time by mutual agreement between the parties, that have been released by ENERGOUS to production, including all Product Updates, which will be deemed to have been added to Exhibit A automatically, without any further action required by the parties, immediately following the release to production date.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.21 "Products" means only the ENERGOUS products specifically listed and exhaustively set forth in Exhibit A, and under no condition should it extend to Product Updates regardless of their release status, without a new written agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Products' includes ENERGOUS products in Exhibit A as amended, explicitly adding Product Updates automatically. The modified definition excludes Product Updates unless through new written agreements. This in-text contradiction could impact sections like 2.1, License Grant, where the extent of the license on Product Updates becomes uncertain because the definition of product is being contradicted. The contradiction is that product updates are no longer automatically considered Products, however, under exhibit A, it indicates as well that Products includes 'any related Product Updates'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.21 "Products" means only the ENERGOUS products specifically listed and exhaustively set forth in Exhibit A, and under no condition should it extend to Product Updates regardless of their release status, without a new written agreement.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of 'Products' explicitly excludes 'Product Updates' unless through new written agreements, directly contradicting the original definition, which included them automatically as part of Exhibit A. The contradicted text is, in this case, identical to the Changed Text. The original inclusion of Product Updates as part of the 'Products' definition, as stated in the original text, is now being excluded by the Change Text, which causes the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 11.2 Product Warranty. (a) ENERGOUS warrants that (i) when manufactured in accordance with the Approved Production Specifications, and as implemented in a suitable circuit application in accordance with the Product Specifications, the Products and Product Die will conform to the Product Specifications and will be free from defects that could have been avoided in their design; (ii) the Products, any constituent parts or functionality thereof, the Documentation and the Deposit Materials do not infringe any third party's Intellectual Property Rights; (iii) it did not misappropriate any third party's trade secrets in the development of the Products, any constituent parts or functionality thereof, the Documentation or the Deposit Materials; and (iv) when delivered (including any software updates if any), no Product will contain any viruses, "Trojan horses" or other harmful code. The above warranties are valid for a period of [***] from the date of shipment of any Licensed Product to any customer.
perturbation.changed_text: 11.2 Product Warranty. (a) ENERGOUS warrants that (i) when manufactured in accordance with the Approved Production Specifications, and as implemented in a suitable circuit application in accordance with the Product Specifications, the Products and Product Die will conform to the Product Specifications and will be free from defects that could have been avoided in their design; (ii) the Products, any constituent parts or functionality thereof, the Documentation and the Deposit Materials do not infringe any third party's Intellectual Property Rights; (iii) it did not misappropriate any third party's trade secrets in the development of the Products, any constituent parts or functionality thereof, the Documentation or the Deposit Materials; and (iv) when delivered (excluding any software updates), no Product will contain any viruses, "Trojan horses" or other harmful code. The above warranties are valid for a period of [***] from the date of shipment of any Licensed Product to any customer.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'including any software updates if any' to 'excluding any software updates', we introduce a direct contradiction in Section 11.2. This creates ambiguity regarding ENERGOUS's liability for defects related to software updates. Now, the clause (iv) indicates that ENERGOUS does not warrant any updates if any for the products, in contradiction with the opening sentence that defines ENERGOUS warrants Products will conform to the Product Specifications.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.2 Product Warranty. (a) ENERGOUS warrants that (i) when manufactured in accordance with the Approved Production Specifications, and as implemented in a suitable circuit application in accordance with the Product Specifications, the Products and Product Die will conform to the Product Specifications and will be free from defects that could have been avoided in their design; (ii) the Products, any constituent parts or functionality thereof, the Documentation and the Deposit Materials do not infringe any third party's Intellectual Property Rights; (iii) it did not misappropriate any third party's trade secrets in the development of the Products, any constituent parts or functionality thereof, the Documentation or the Deposit Materials; and (iv) when delivered (excluding any software updates), no Product will contain any viruses, "Trojan horses" or other harmful code. The above warranties are valid for a period of [***] from the date of shipment of any Licensed Product to any customer.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'including any software updates if any' to 'excluding any software updates' directly contradicts the initial warranty statement in Section 11.2, which states ENERGOUS warrants Products will conform to the Product Specifications. By explicitly excluding software updates, the modified text creates ambiguity and inconsistency regarding the extent of ENERGOUS's warranty obligations, especially considering the original intent to warrant products will conform to the Product Specifications.
